title: Phyllorkis patens
intro: Phyllorkis patens is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia, known for its unique flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis patens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Phyllorkis patens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Phyllorkis patens belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. Phyllorkis patens is native to tropical Africa. It is a type of orchid that thrives in shaded environments. The species is also known as the 'Yellow-flowered Phyllorkis'.
